joints and separation joints at all levels and wherever specified to the required sizes, shapes, etc., complete.The rate is including
all kind of wastages, lead and Lifts etc.

Additional threading maked at column termination place.Providing and fixing Mechanical Splicing using threaded coupler to the
reinforcement bars of specified dia and grade using high yield strength standard couplers of specially produced steel to join
ribbed bearing bars machine cut end to end. Cost to include necessary supply of threaded couplers of various diameters,
reducing diameters,transportation,coupling at site by mechanical means, threading of reinforcement bars at ends,erection at
site, testing of random samples, testing of joints at site using ultrasonic methods required by Structural consultant at intervals as
directed, protection of threaded ends using PVC covers, all tools and tackels, lead and lift etc., complete at all levels and
location specified as per drawings.Cost to include necessary testing of couplers as per IS 16172 CRE- MS Couplers - 32mm dia
CRE- Reducer Couplers - 32 to 20 mm dia
CRE- Reducer Couplers - 25 to 20 mm dia

STP Hard rock excavation (Grid-(ML-M to ML-P)Specification & scope of work same as 1.01 of the BOQ but for Excavation in
Hard rock / boulders (more than 1/2 Cum in size -Un poclainable) by chiselling/ wedging to required level for Raft, Footings of
columns, Retaining walls, Core walls, catch pits, Lift pits,STP,UG tanks, trenches for drains, etc. to the required depth including
all leads and lifts, dressing, grading, cleaning, excavating loose pockets directed and disposing etc. as per detailed specification
and conforming to safety rules of Authority during the progress of work.Note: The percentage of voids to be deducted from stack
measurement is 40% for hardrock or Initial levels will be taken before blasting hard rock and final levels will be taken after
achieving required levels in presence of PIC.

Lift WALLS WATERPROOFING WORKS- (EXTERNAL) - Lift Walls - External walls (Extra over and above rate of item no-7.2-I
of the BOQ for Lift External Wall Waterproofing Works) 1. Surface preparation: Before starting of waterproofing works the
surface of the wall should be made free of any loose particles, vegetation, grease or oily matters etc. All tie rod holes should be
filled properly. All honeycombs should be repaired as per approved repair procedures. Treating the Construction Joints : Chase
opening the Construction Joints by providing ''V " groove, removing the dust and clean the designated area following by
providing with approved nonshrink high strength cemenitious grout, finishing, curing etc. as per manufacturers specification and
as directed. Any damp spots on the walls should be attended by grouting cement mixed with flow enhancers like Cebex-100 or
equivalent until the leak or dampness is fully arrested. When PVC pipes are inserted in the designated openings in the slab, it
should be applied with epoxy bonding agent sprayed with sand on the portion coming in contact with non shrink grout or mortar
or concrete to enhance the bonding and packed with non shrink grout includiing scafolding as directed.All sharp edges and
corners to be chamferred / haunched to receive the membrane.2. Providing and laying minimum 1.5 mm thick self adhesive, self
healing type SBS based membranes minimum puncture resistance of 220N(ASTM E154), tensile resistance over 200%,
Elongation over 200%(ASTM D-412) and tear resistance of more than 200N (ASTM D1004). The membrane shall be laid over a
compatible primer as per Manufacturer's specification with sufficient overlaps.3. This membrane should be protected with 250
GSM minimum non woven polyester mat for high puncture resistance laid over the membrane either loosely laid or fixed as per
manufacturer specifications.4. The SBS membrane as well as the protection layer should have sufficient overlap as per
manufacturer specifications.5. The applicator/manufacturer should submit the details of waterproofing near the penetrations on
the wall.6. The membrane should be terminated on all sides properly with watertight joints. 7.The manufacturer/applicator
should submit the details and get client approval prior to start the work.8.The contractor to include cost of termination of
waterproofing system with necessary overlapping, joint treatment etc complete all as per manufacturer's
recommendations.9.Treatment shall be provided from standard manufacturers like BASF, SIKA, FOSROC or equivalent.Lift
Walls - External walls (Extra over and above rate of item no-7.2-i (BOQ)
Dewatering in Construction site various locations (Drain water & Underground water uplift in the construction site)

column1:
Terms & Conditions
SPECIFIC CLARIFICATIONS
The intent of these clauses is to amplify the provisions in the tender documents, summarise the agreed clarifications, qualifications and assumptions stated in the
tender submissions and discussed during the meetings.
The specific clarifications made here under should be read with the respective conditions of the contract. The balance provisions of the respective clauses shall
continue to remain in force.
1. GCC - Appendix to tender 8.1: Commencement date: ‘Commencement date for the Project’ shall be the date of LOI.
2. GCC - Appendix to tender 1.1.3.3, 1.1.5.6, 8.2: Time for Construction:
Phase 1 comprising of 7 Towers, Club House, MLCP, External works & other associated works
"Completion date / duration for Phase 1" shall be within 28 months from the ‘Commencement date of the Project’ which is date of LOI.
Phase 2 comprising of 2 Towers & other associated works
Phase 2 works shall initiate from the date specified in the ‘Notice to Proceed for Phase 2 works’ issued by the Client.
"Completion date / duration for Phase2" shall be within 36 months from the ‘Commencement date of the Project’ which is date of LOI.
Overall project duration (For both phase 1 & phase 2) shall be within 36 months from the ‘Commencement date of the Project’ which is date of LOI.
